Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 3, 080 [Namburbu][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

MUŠEN a-ḫu-u₂ ina E₂ NA IGI⸢ir⸣

(o 1) (If) a strange bird appears in the house of a man: that house's full storage vessels will be empty. The same (a strange bird appears in the house of a man): that estate will be squandered.

o 22

E₂ BI I₃.DUB-MEŠ-šu₂ SA₅-MEŠ ⸢SU₃-MEŠ⸣ KI.MIN<(MUŠEN)> a-ḫu-u₂ ina E₂ NA IGIir E₂ BI BIRaḫ

o 33

NAM.BUR₂.BI ḪUL MUŠEN BARi ana LU₂ u E₂-šu₂ la₃ TEe

(o 3) Apotropaic ritual for the evil of a strange bird not to approach a man and his house.

o 44

DU₃.DU₃-BI ina U₄ ŠE.GA liq-te-diš UR₃ SAR ⸢A?⸣ KU₃ ⸢SU₃⸣ 3 GI.DU₈

(o 4) Its ritual: On a propitious day he should purify himself. You sweep the roof. You sprinkle pure water. You set up 3 offering tables for Ea, Šamaš and Asalluhi. You put down 3 rations, each of 12 loaves (made) from isqūqu-flour. You strew dates (and) saskû-flour. You put down a cake (made) from honey (and) butter. You set up an adagurru-vessel. You libate first-class beer.

o 55

ana de₂-a dUTU u dASAR.LU₂.ḪI GUBan 3 KURUM₆-MEŠ 12-TA.AM₃

o 66

⸢NINDA!(7) ZI₃.KUM GARan ZU₂.LUM.MA zi₃EŠA DUBaq NINDA.I₃.DE₂.A

o 77

LAL₃ I₃.NUN.NA GARan dugA.DA.GUR₅ GUBan KAŠ SAG BALqi₂

o 88

NIG₂.NA šimLI GARan uduSISKUR₂ BALqi₂

(o 8) You put down a censer (full) of juniper. You present an offering.

o 99

LU₂ ša₂ MUŠEN a-ḫu-u ana E₂-šu₂ KU₄ ana UGU TUG₂-šu₂ TUG₂ GE₆ ŠA₃.ḪA MU₄.MU₄aš

(o 9) The man into whose house the strange bird came puts on a black garment (made) of linen over his clothing.

o 1010

šum-ma MUŠEN ia₂-a-nu tam-šil MUŠEN šu-a-tu₂ ša₂ IM DU₃uš

(o 10) If the (actual) bird is not available, you make an image of that bird from clay.

o 1111

ki-ma šu₂-a-tu₂ u₂-bar-ram-šu₂-ma ina TE KEŠ₂ ana GUB₃ GARan

(o 11) He makes it multicoloured like that (actual bird) and you put (it) down at the side of the ritual assemblage on the left.

o 1212

⸢KU₃.SI₂₂ ḪUŠ⸣.A 15 ŠE KU₃.BABBAR LU₂ IL₂-ma kam DU₁₁.GA

(o 12) The man raises red gold (and) 15 grains of silver and speaks as follows.

o 1313

DINGIR-⸢MEŠ GAL-MEŠ⸣ ba-nu-u₂ DINGIR-MEŠ u LU₂ at-tu-nu

(o 13) Great gods, you are the creators of the (junior) gods and of man.

o 1414

⸢e-ṭi-ru⸣ [ZI]⸢ti₃⸣ mu-bal-li-ṭu lu₂UŠ₂

(o 14) Saviours of life, revivers of the dead!

o 1515

pa-ṭi-ru ka-si-i za-[qi₂]-⸢pu ma-aq⸣-tu₂ mu-še-ti-iq U₂.DA

(o 15) Releasers of the captive, raisers of the fallen, ones who make afflictions pass by!

o 1616

ana ba-⸢laṭ⸣ ZI-ME-ia KU₃.BABBAR ip-ṭi-ri-ia₂ IL₂-ku-nu-ši

(o 16) For the good health of my life I raise to you the silver for my ransom.

o 1717

lim-ḫu-ru-in-ni-ma ina DU₁₁.GA-ku-nu ana-ku lu-⸢ub₂⸣-luṭ

(o 17) May they (the gods) receive me and at your command let me live.

o 1818

an-nam 7-šu₂ DU₁₁.GA-ma ša₂ IL₂u₂ ina IGI KEŠ₂

(o 18) He says this 7 times and he puts down what he raises in the middle in front of the ritual assemblage. If the (actual) bird is not at hand, he raises the image of the bird, and he kneels down to the left of the ritual assemblage, and he speaks whatever afflicts his heart.

o 1919

ina ŠA₃ GARan šum-ma MUŠEN ⸢la₃ GAL₂?⸣-ma tam-šil MUŠEN IL₂-ma

o 2020

ina GUB₃ KEŠ₂ DU₁₀.GAM-ma ma-la ŠA₃-šu₂ DABtu₄ DU₁₁.DU₁₁ub

o 2121

GIM KEŠ₂ it-tu-ḫu kam DU₁₁.GA

(o 21) When the ritual assemblage has stopped he speaks as follows.

o 2222

EN₂ de₂-a dUTU u dAMAR.UTU DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ

(o 22) Incantation: Ea, Šamaš and Marduk, great gods, you are the ones who decree fates.

o 2323

mu-šim NAM-MEŠ at-tu-nu

o 2424

muš-te-nu-u₂ A₂-MEŠ GISKIM-MEŠ ḪUL-MEŠ

(o 24) Ones who reassign ominous conditions (and) evil signs!

o 2525

u₂-še-ti-qu U₂.DA ḪUL-MEŠ ṣa-bi-tu ŠU na-as-ki

(o 25) They, the ones who hold the hand of the abandoned (in support), make evil afflictions pass by.

o 2626

de-ku-u₂ ma-aq-tu mu-bal-li-ṭu lu₂GIG

(o 26) Raisers of the fallen, revivers of the sick!

o 2727

mu-šal-li-mu ḫab-li₃ ša₂ šu-lum ZIti₃ it-ti-šu₂ la₃ ba-šu-u₂

(o 27) Deliverers of a wronged man who is without well-being in his life!

o 2828

at-tu-nu-ma DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ DI.KU₅ ša₂ ANe u KIti₃

(o 28) You are the great gods, the judges of heaven and earth.

o 2929

i-ziz-za-nim-ma ina TU₁₅-ku-nu DU₁₀.GA šu-ti-⸢qa⸣-[in-ni]

(o 29) Stand by me and with your sweet breath cause (afflictions) to pass me by.

Reverse
r 1r 1

ina qi₂-bi-ti-ku-nu DINGIR ze-na-a diš₈-tar₂ ze-ni-[ti]

(r 1) At your command may the angry god (and) the angry goddess, who are angry with me and have placed on me ominous conditions (and) evil signs, and the terrifying dreams that I keep on seeing, my dreams are very dark, my ominous conditions very bad -- at your command, great gods, may the angry god (and) the angry goddess be at peace with me on this day.

r 22

ša₂ it-ti-ia₂ ze-nu-ma A₂-MEŠ GISKIM-MEŠ ḪUL-MEŠ iš-ku-nu-ni

r 33

u₃ MAŠ₂.GE₆-MEŠ par₂-da-a-ta ša₂ at-ta-aṭ-ṭa-lu

r 44

⸢uk⸣-ku-lu MAŠ₂.GE₆-MEŠ-u₂-a lum-mu-na i-da-tu-u₂-a

r 55

ina DU₁₁.GA-ku-nu DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ DINGIR ze-na-a diš₈-tar₂ ze-ni-ti

r 66

ina u₄-mu an-ni-i li-is-li-mu it-ti-ia₂

r 77

ina ḪUL MUŠEN an-ni-i ša₂ ia-a-ši iš-šak-nam-ma

(r 7) On account of the evil of this bird that has been placed on me and -- I am frightened, I am afraid and I am constantly terrified -- appeared in my house and allows no rest in the night and all of the day: stand by me, great gods, in my case, and cause (afflictions) to pass me by from the evil of this bird, and may I not die, may I not be treated unjustly.

r 88

pal-ḫa-ku ad-ra-ku u šu-ta-du-ra-ku

r 99

ina E₂-ia₅ IGIru-ma ina mu-ši u kal u₄-mi la u₂-šap-ša₂-ḫu

r 1010

DINGIR-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ ina di-ni-ia₅ i-ziz-za-nim-ma

r 1111

ina ḪUL MUŠEN an-ni-i šu-ti-qa-in-ni-ma la a-ma-ti la aḫ-ḫab-bil

r 1212

de₂-a dUTU u dAMAR.UTU dup-pi-ra lum-ni

(r 12) Ea, Šamaš and Marduk, withdraw my evil.

r 1313

ina DU₁₁.GA-ku-nu li-ti-qa-an-ni-ma da₃-li₂-li₂-ku-nu lud-lul

(r 13) At your command may it pass me by and may I sing your praises, and may those who see me in the street sing praise of your great divinity.

r 1414

u a-mi-ru-u₂-a ina SILA da-lil DINGIRti-ku-nu GALtu₂ lid-lu-lu

r 1515

ku-um-mu ana-ku de₂-a EN ba-na ⸢LU₂?ti?⸣ [x] x x dUTU DI.KU₅

(r 15) I am yours, Ea, lord, creator of humanity, [...] ..., Šamaš,judge.

r 1616

šu-bar-a-a šu-kun ⸢ABGAL⸣ DINGIR-MEŠ dAMAR.UTU

(r 16) Bring about my freedom, sage of the gods, Marduk.

r 1717

an-nam 7-šu₂ DU₁₁.GA-ma lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ U₂ gišKIRI₆ ka-la-šu₂-nu

(r 17) He says this 7 times and the mašmaššu-priest wipes him clean with all the plants from the orchard. You move the censer (and) the torch past him.

r 1818

u₂-kap-par-šu₂ NIG₂.NA GI.IZI.LA₂ tuš-ba-aʾ-šu₂

r 1919

U₂-MEŠ ša₂ ŠU lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ i-na-ʾi-is MUŠEN IL₂-ma

(r 19) He chews the plants from the hand of the mašmaššu-priest. He raises the bird and he goes into the grave of a dead man and he puts that bird down by the dead man and he does not look behind him. At the stipulated time he collects bread from the hand of the cook (or) baker, he collects beer from the hand of the brewer.

r 2020

ana KI.MAḪ lu₂UŠ₂ KU₄-ma MUŠEN šu-a-tu₂ KI lu₂UŠ₂ GARan-ma

r 2121

a-na EGIR-šu₂ NU IGI.BAR ina u₄-me-šu-ma ina ŠU lu₂MUḪALDIM

r 2222

e-pi-i NINDA i-maḫ-ḫar ina ŠU lu₂KURUN.NAM KAŠ i-maḫ-ḫar

r 2323

ina KA₂ E₂ lu₂NU.gišKIRI₆ kušE.SIR₂ GUB₃-šu₂ i-ša₂-ḫaṭ

(r 23) He takes off his left shoe at the door of the gardener's house.

r 2424

ina KA₂ e₂EŠ₂.DAM ser₃-qam DUBaq

(r 24) He strews an offering at the door of the tavern.

r 2525

tug₂par₂-ši-gu ŠU.GUR ZABAR ŠUBdi-ma

(r 25) He lays down a headdress (and) a ring of bronze, and he does not go along the street that he went along. He goes straight to his house.

r 2626

⸢SILA DIB⸣ NU DIB.BA ana E₂-šu₂ iš-ši-ir

(colophon)
r 2727

GABA.RI gišDA GABA.RI KA₂.DINGIR.RAki SAR-ma ba-ri

(r 27) Copy of a writing-board, copy from Babylon, written and checked.

r 2828

DUB md30—ba-nu-nu lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ DUMU mDA—dAMAR.UTU

(r 28) Tablet of Sin-banunu, mašmaššu-priest, son of Ileʾʾi-Marduk.
